pr.title: Add per-tool agent context files (Claude / Gemini / Replit)
pr.body:
## Summary

Four small files that make this repo work cleanly across Claude Code, Gemini CLI / Gemini Code Assist, and Replit Agent — without duplicating documentation that already exists.

| File | Auto-loaded by | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Claude Code | 27 |
| `GEMINI.md` | Gemini CLI + Gemini Code Assist | 27 |
| `replit.md` | Replit Agent | 32 |
| `.replit` | Replit workspace runtime | 23 |

## How they work

Each `.md` has an identical "read these first" section pointing at the existing canonical docs:

- `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- `docs/handoff.md`
- `docs/architecture.md`
- `docs/adr/`

…rather than duplicating their content. When `CONTRIBUTING.md` changes, no per-tool file needs updating.

What each adds beyond the doc pointers:

- **Repo defaults any agent must respect**: lint rules at `error`, the 5 required CI checks, migration filename format, the no-monster-files rule (~700-line cap), the Zod-on-server-actions convention. The things that would silently fail CI or get rejected at review if an agent didn't know about them up-front.
- **replit.md additionally**: how to run THIS repo on Replit specifically — Secrets panel mapping, Node version source, and the fact that Replit is a dev environment only (production is Cloudflare Workers, not Replit Deployments).

## `.replit` specifics

- `run = "npm run dev:next"` — Replit's **Run** button boots the dev server out-of-the-box.
- `modules = ["nodejs-22:v0"]` matches `.nvmrc`.
- `[deployment]` block points at `npm run build` / `npm run start` so Replit's Deploy button surfaces a sane command rather than its default (which would conflict with our OpenNext output).
- Port 3000 → external 80.

## What's NOT in this PR

- **No content duplication.** The temptation with these files is to copy-paste your architecture into them. Resisted — the human-readable docs are the source of truth.
- **No tool-specific code or hooks.** This is config + context only.
- **No changes to CONTRIBUTING.md or other docs.** They're already AI-friendly.

## Test plan

- [x] `tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `npm run lint` 0 errors (7 unchanged Phase B warnings)
- [x] `npm test` 152 / 152 pass
- [x] `npm run format:check` clean
- [ ] CI green
- [ ] Manual smoke: open the repo in Claude Code (already running), Gemini CLI, and Replit. Each should auto-load its file and orient itself without further prompting.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
pr.author: zakaria-bennis
